etymology.description: 屮(屮) (chè, "sprout") + 八(八) (bā, "eight") = various plants emerging
etymology.interpretation: The character 卉 (huì, "plant") combines 屮(屮) (chè, "sprout"), depicting vegetation, with 八(八) (bā, "eight"), implying multiplicity or variety. Together, they convey the idea of numerous plants or a diversity of vegetation growing forth, reflecting the character's use to represent 'plant.'

examples.chinese: 这个花园里有各种各样的卉(這個花園裏有各種各樣的卉)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège huāyuán lǐ yǒu gèzhǒng gè yàng de huì.
examples.english: This garden has all kinds of plants.

examples.chinese: 卉的种类繁多(卉的種類繁多)，让人眼花缭乱(讓人眼花繚亂)。
examples.pinyin: Huì de zhǒnglèi fánduō, ràng rén yǎnhuā-liáoluàn.
examples.english: The variety of plants is overwhelming, making people dizzy.

examples.chinese: 科学家正在研究新的卉品种(科學家正在研究新的卉品種)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiā zhèngzài yánjiū xīn de huì pǐnzhǒng.
examples.english: Scientists are researching new plant species.
